name: Privacy by Design
def: Embedding privacy protections into systems from the outset — data minimisation, purpose limitation, and user control — rather than bolting them on afterward.
problem: Privacy added late is superficial; systems that collect and retain data by default create breach risk and regulatory exposure (GDPR/CCPA).
mechanism: Collect only what is needed (minimisation), use it only for declared purposes, default to the most private settings, anonymise / pseudonymise where possible, set retention limits with deletion, and make privacy a first-class requirement and default in the design.
benefits: Reduces breach impact and regulatory risk
whenNot: Systems handling no personal data (still good hygiene)
myths: "Privacy and functionality are zero-sum" — minimisation often improves both security and clarity
